articles.title: Elon Musk appeared more petty than prepared
articles.summary: Elon Musk appeared unprepared and flat during his testimony in Musk v. Altman, showing little animation compared to his previous courtroom appearances.

articles.title: Elon Musk tells the jury that all he wants to do is save humanity
articles.summary: Elon Musk testified in his lawsuit against OpenAI co-founder Sam Altman, positioning himself as humanity's savior while recounting his background from South Africa through his various business ventures.

articles.title: Taylor Swift is stepping up the legal war on AI copycats
articles.summary: Taylor Swift is escalating legal efforts against AI copycats through trademark applications to protect her spoken phrases. However, experts suggest her legal approach may face significant challenges.

articles.title: At his OpenAI trial, Musk relitigates an old friendship
articles.summary: Musk testified under oath at his OpenAI trial, recounting a previously shared account of his friendship history with the company's founders.

articles.title: Amazon is already offering new OpenAI products on AWS
articles.summary: AWS now offers OpenAI products after Microsoft ended exclusivity. Amazon announced multiple OpenAI model offerings including a new agent service.

articles.title: Amazon launches an AI-powered audio Q&A experience on product pages
articles.summary: Amazon launched an AI-powered audio Q&A feature on product pages, allowing customers to ask questions and receive spoken responses about products.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a cloud platform with 2 million developers, raised $100 million to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud. The funding highlights developer demand for simpler, cost-effective alternatives to legacy cloud platforms.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code's $20-$200 monthly pricing has prompted developers to seek alternatives. Goose, an open-source AI coding agent by Block, offers similar functionality completely free and locally.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B funding after a viral billboard campaign featuring AI token puzzles that attracted thousands of engineering candidates. The creative hiring stunt decoded to a coding challenge, successfully helping the startup recruit talent in competitive AI market.

articles.title: Open source package with 1 million monthly downloads stole user credentials
articles.summary: A compromised open source package with 1 million monthly downloads was exploited to steal user credentials including cloud keys and API tokens. Attackers accessed signing keys through a vulnerability, releasing a malicious version that harvested sensitive data.

articles.title: Why are top university websites serving porn? It comes down to shoddy housekeeping.
articles.summary: Top university websites including Berkeley, Columbia, and Washington University are serving pornography and malicious content due to poor subdomain management by administrators. Hundreds of subdomains across 34+ universities have been exploited by scammers.

articles.title: In a first, a ransomware family is confirmed to be quantum-safe
articles.summary: Kyber ransomware claims to use quantum-safe ML-KEM encryption to protect against quantum computer attacks. This marks the first confirmed ransomware family using post-quantum cryptography standards.

articles.title: The Download: Musk and Altman’s legal showdown, and AI’s profit problem
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face trial over OpenAI's future, with Musk seeking $134 billion in damages and demanding the company return to non-profit status ahead of its IPO.

articles.title: Elon Musk and Sam Altman are going to court over OpenAI’s future
articles.summary: Elon Musk and Sam Altman face trial over OpenAI's transition to for-profit status. The court could determine if the company can operate as a for-profit enterprise and potentially remove current leadership ahead of OpenAI's anticipated IPO.

articles.title: The missing step between hype and profit
articles.summary: AI development lacks clear steps between hype and practical profit, with activists questioning the missing middle ground. A London protest highlighted uncertainty about AI's trajectory and benefits.

articles.title: Introducing NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni: Long-Context Multimodal Intelligence for Documents, Audio and Video Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA released Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a multimodal AI model handling documents, audio, and video with extended context capabilities for intelligent agents.

articles.title: How to build scalable web apps with OpenAI's Privacy Filter
articles.summary: OpenAI's Privacy Filter enables developers to build scalable web applications while protecting user data. The tool filters sensitive information before processing, ensuring privacy compliance at scale.

articles.title: DeepSeek-V4: a million-token context that agents can actually use
articles.summary: DeepSeek-V4 introduces a million-token context window that enables AI agents to process and utilize vast amounts of information effectively, advancing practical applications of large language models.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses future directions in AI development and emerging trends in artificial intelligence technology.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands like Hot Wheels leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity in content creation and marketing strategies.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft released its 'farm of the future' toolkit as open source, enabling broader access to agricultural technology and AI-driven farming solutions for sustainable food production.

articles.title: Celebrating 20 years of Google Translate: Fun facts, tips and new features to try
articles.summary: Google Translate celebrates 20 years of service, evolving from a 2006 AI experiment to supporting nearly 250 languages. The company shares fun facts and new features marking this milestone.

articles.title: Join the new AI Agents Vibe Coding Course from Google and Kaggle
articles.summary: Google and Kaggle are offering a 5-Day AI Agents Intensive Course with registration now open. This vibe coding course provides hands-on training in AI agent development.

articles.title: 8 Gemini tips for organizing your space (and life)
articles.summary: Gemini offers AI-powered tips for organizing physical and digital spaces, including cleaning schedules, inbox decluttering, and seasonal chore management.

articles.title: Announcing our partnership with the Republic of Korea
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with South Korea to accelerate scientific breakthroughs using frontier AI models.

articles.title: Decoupled DiLoCo: A new frontier for resilient, distributed AI training
articles.summary: Decoupled DiLoCo introduces a new method for resilient distributed AI training that improves fault tolerance and efficiency. The approach decouples communication from computation, enabling more robust training across multiple systems.

articles.title: Partnering with industry leaders to accelerate AI transformation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind partners with global consultancies to help organizations implement frontier AI technology. This collaboration aims to accelerate AI transformation across industries worldwide.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family, while discussions covered Mamba 3 and attention residuals in AI development.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia launches DLSS 5, a generative AI technology that enhances video game graphics in real-time with photorealistic improvements. OpenAI develops a superapp while MiniMax releases M2.7 model.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive features from ChatGPT and Anthropic for math/science visualization.

articles.title: IBM launches AI platform Bob to regulate SDLC costs
articles.summary: IBM launches Bob, an AI platform designed to control software development lifecycle costs and governance while maintaining speed. It integrates governance and security measures into the development process to prevent unmanaged liabilities from coding assistants.

articles.title: The evolution of encoders: From simple models to multimodal AI
articles.summary: Encoders have evolved from simple data converters to sophisticated multimodal systems that help AI understand various forms of information. This transformation enables machines to process and interpret real-world data effectively.

articles.title: Kakao Mobility details Level 4 autonomous driving roadmap for physical AI
articles.summary: Kakao Mobility unveiled its Level 4 autonomous driving development roadmap as part of its physical AI strategy, aiming to integrate autonomous technologies with infrastructure for mobility services in Korea.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke develop methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, improving system reliability and debugging.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, self-positioning, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: Claude Code is leaking API keys into public package registries
articles.summary: A study reveals that AI coding assistants like Claude Code are unintentionally leaking sensitive API keys to public code repositories and package registries.

articles.title: Anthropic’s MCP vulnerability: When ‘expected behavior’ becomes a supply chain nightmare
articles.summary: Security researchers discovered a critical architectural flaw in Anthropic's Model Context Protocol that could enable remote takeovers of millions of AI applications, creating significant supply chain risks.

articles.title: The paradox of LLM self-distillation: Faster reasoning, weaker generalization
articles.summary: Self-distillation optimizes LLMs for speed but reduces their ability to explore alternative solutions on complex problems, revealing a critical tradeoff between efficiency and generalization.

articles.title: smol-audio: A Colab-Friendly Notebook Collection for Fine-Tuning Whisper, Parakeet, Voxtral, Granite Speech, and Audio Flamingo 3
articles.summary: smol-audio provides Google Colab notebooks for fine-tuning advanced audio AI models like Whisper, Parakeet, and Audio Flamingo 3, addressing the lack of accessible practical guidance for working with state-of-the-art audio models.

articles.title: A Coding Implementation on Document Parsing Benchmarking with LlamaIndex ParseBench Using Python, Hugging Face, and Evaluation Metrics
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates evaluating document parsing systems using ParseBench dataset from Hugging Face with LlamaIndex. It covers loading data, building baseline extraction pipelines with PyMuPDF, and preparing inputs for advanced OCR and vision-language models.

articles.title: Poolside AI Introduces Laguna XS.2 and M.1: Agentic Coding Models Reaching 68.2% and 72.5% on SWE-bench Verified
articles.summary: Poolside AI released Laguna M.1 and XS.2, Mixture-of-Experts coding models achieving 68.2% and 72.5% on SWE-bench Verified. The company also released pool, a lightweight coding agent tool for internal RL training.

articles.title: Over 80% of US government agencies already use AI agents - and it's only the beginning
articles.summary: Over 80% of US government agencies currently use AI agents, with leaders expecting increased human-AI collaboration by 2030 in the public sector.

articles.title: Microsoft finally open sources DOS 1.0 - and it's so much more than the code
articles.summary: Microsoft open-sourced DOS 1.0, providing rare insight into early PC history and the company's foundational operating system code.

articles.title: I was not expecting a Razer keyboard to enhance my office productivity - here's how it did
articles.summary: Razer's Pro Type Ergo keyboard surprised with its split ergonomic design and wrist rest, boosting office productivity while maintaining gaming capabilities.

articles.title: Effective Context Engineering for AI Agents: A Developer’s Guide
articles.summary: Guide on context engineering for AI agents, helping developers optimize how agents process and use contextual information for better performance and accuracy.

articles.title: Text Summarization with Scikit-LLM
articles.summary: Scikit-LLM enables text summarization using scikit-learn integration with large language models, simplifying NLP tasks for developers.

articles.title: Building AI Agents with Local Small Language Models
articles.summary: Building AI agents is now accessible to everyone with local small language models, not just large tech companies. This democratization enables individuals and smaller organizations to create custom AI agents.

articles.title: Let the AI Do the Experimenting
articles.summary: AI autoresearch optimizes marketing campaigns while managing budget constraints efficiently. The approach allows automated experimentation to improve campaign performance.

articles.title: Correlation Doesn’t Mean Causation! But What Does It Mean?
articles.summary: An exploration of what correlation actually tells us in data analysis, clarifying the distinction between correlation and causation in statistical relationships.

articles.title: The Next Frontier of AI in Production Is Chaos Engineering
articles.summary: AI systems need chaos engineering to understand failure impacts and learn from breakdowns. Blast-radius control exists but intent-based tooling remains immature for production AI.

articles.title: NVIDIA Launches Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Model, Unifying Vision, Audio and Language for up to 9x More Efficient AI Agents
articles.summary: NVIDIA launched Nemotron 3 Nano Omni, a unified multimodal model combining vision, audio, and language capabilities into one system for 9x more efficient AI agents with faster processing and advanced reasoning.

articles.title: Into the Omniverse: Manufacturing’s Simulation-First Era Has Arrived
articles.summary: Manufacturing is entering a simulation-first era using NVIDIA Omniverse and OpenUSD, moving away from reliance on physical testing toward virtual design-build-test cycles.

articles.title: OpenAI’s New GPT-5.5 Powers Codex on NVIDIA Infrastructure — and NVIDIA Is Already Putting It to Work
articles.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.5 powers Codex, an agentic coding application running on NVIDIA's GB200 infrastructure. Over 10,000 NVIDIA employees across departments report significant productivity gains using the new model.

articles.title: Migrating a text agent to a voice assistant with Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
articles.summary: Amazon Nova 2 Sonic enables migration of text agents to voice assistants for natural, real-time speech interactions. The article explores architecture, design priorities, and best practices for building conversational voice assistants across industries.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni model now available on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni multimodal model is now available on Amazon SageMaker JumpStart, enabling enterprises to process video, audio, images, and text simultaneously for intelligent applications.

articles.title: Automate repetitive tasks with Amazon Quick Flows
articles.summary: Amazon Quick Flows automates repetitive business tasks using AI-powered workflows built with natural language, eliminating manual work like data copying and reporting without requiring coding expertise.

articles.title: Scaling Biomolecular Modeling Using Context Parallelism in NVIDIA BioNeMo
articles.summary: NVIDIA BioNeMo uses context parallelism to overcome GPU memory limitations in biomolecular modeling, enabling researchers to study complex biological systems without fragmenting them into isolated components.

articles.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Nano Omni Powers Multimodal Agent Reasoning in a Single Efficient Open Model
articles.summary: NVIDIA's Nemotron 3 Nano Omni is an efficient open model enabling multimodal agent reasoning across vision, audio, and text in a single unified system, eliminating the need for fragmented model chains.

articles.title: 24/7 Simulation Loops: How Agentic AI Keeps Subsurface Engineering Moving
articles.summary: Agentic AI enables 24/7 continuous simulation loops for subsurface engineering, automating manual workflows that previously required expert intervention and addressing the growing gap between data complexity and human capacity.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines how different generations perceive and respond to social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta commits to net zero emissions by 2030, developing technology to reduce its carbon footprint and sharing these solutions openly.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with an unconventional background successfully developed innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how diverse experiences can drive breakthrough advancements in cutting-edge fields.

articles.title: Gradient-based Planning for World Models at Longer Horizons
articles.summary: GRASP is a gradient-based planner for learned world models enabling long-horizon planning through parallel trajectory optimization, stochastic exploration, and gradient reshaping for cleaner action signals.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers present methods for identifying feature interactions at scale in Large Language Models to improve interpretability and transparency. The work analyzes LLM decision-making through feature attribution to advance AI safety and trustworthiness.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research on information-driven imaging systems that use encoders and noise models to optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. The approach quantifies imaging system performance through information estimation from noisy measurements.
